summary: The 2JZ-GTE is one of the most reliable performance engines ever built. The cast iron block handles enormous power, and the rest of the drivetrain is overbuilt for the era.

issues.name: Twin-Turbo System Complexity
issues.description: The sequential twin-turbo system has many vacuum lines, solenoids, and actuators that fail with age. The turbo transition at 4,000 RPM can become rough or disappear entirely.

issues.name: Brittle Wiring Harness
issues.description: The 30+ year old wiring harness becomes brittle and develops cracks in the insulation, causing intermittent electrical faults.

issues.name: V160 Transmission Weakness
issues.description: The Getrag V160 6-speed transmission synchros can wear on modified cars, and replacement transmissions are extremely expensive.

issues.name: Dashboard Cracking
issues.description: The dashboard develops cracks from sun exposure and age. Replacement dashboards are rare and expensive. Most owners use a dash cover.

issues.name: Rubber Seals and Gaskets
issues.description: All rubber components — door seals, valve cover gaskets, coolant hoses — deteriorate with age on a 25+ year old car. Budget for a full refresh.

buyingTips: The 2JZ-GTE is legendary for a reason — it can handle 800+ hp on stock internals with proper supporting mods
Turbo vs NA — only the turbo model has the 2JZ-GTE. The NA model uses the 2JZ-GE which is also reliable but far less valuable
Check for signs of prior modifications — many Supras have been modified and reverted to stock. Look for aftermarket wiring, non-OEM parts
Verify the VIN and production numbers — Supras are expensive enough that fraud exists. Ensure matching VIN plates
Budget for age-related maintenance — any A80 Supra will need rubber, gaskets, and electrical refreshing regardless of mileage
